km.Finistère, Bretagne, France: fiនីsteរe, ប្រេតាហ្គន, ប្រទេសបារាំង

zh.Finistère, Bretagne, France: 法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE於大約1190年出生在法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾時，他的父親Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé27歲，母親Marguerite de Saint Tudy21歲。他和de GUEMENE至少有1個兒子。他去世於大約1225年享年37歲。

zh-hans.Finistère, Bretagne, France: 法国布列塔尼分尼斯艾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE于大约1190年出生在法国布列塔尼分尼斯艾时，他的父亲Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé27岁，母亲Marguerite de Saint Tudy21岁。他和de GUEMENE至少有1个儿子。他去世于大约1225年享年37岁。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: PONT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Pierre.

English (mainly Sussex), Scottish, French, and Catalan: topographic name for someone who lived near a bridge, Middle English, Old French, Catalan pont (from Latin pons, genitive pontis). Compare French Dupont .
Catalan: habitational name from any of numerous places called with pont ‘bridge’.

variants.name: Dupont
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Emile, Jacques, Laurent, Pierre, Adrien, Cecile, Emilien, Gaston, Henri, Alain.

French and Walloon: topographic name for someone ‘from the bridge’, from French pont ‘bridge’ (see Pont ), with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’; or a habitational name for someone from Le Pont, the name of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia).

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Pont) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

nameMeanings.name: L'ABBE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Fernand, Lucien, Normand, Aime, Camille, Emile, Laurent, Marielle, Raoul.

French (Labbé): from abbé, Old French abe(t) ‘priest, abbot’, with fused definite article l', presumably a metonymic occupational name for someone employed in the household of a priest, or a nickname for someone thought to resemble a priest or an abbot in appearance or behavior. Compare Abbe , Labay , Labbie , and Labby .

History: Pierre Labbé dit Lacroix from La Ferté-Bernard in Sarthe, France, married Marguerite Meunier in Beaupré, QC, in 1674. — In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Jean-Baptiste Squerré dit Labbé from Ambres in Tarn, France, who married Marie-Ursule Dauphin in Beauport, QC, in 1730.

variants.name: Labbee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Fernand, Gilles, Marcel.
French (Labbée): altered form of Labbé ‘the priest, the abbot’ (see Labbe ).

variants.name: Labbie
meanings: Altered form of French Labbé ‘the priest, the abbot’ (see Labbe ).

variants.name: Labby
meanings: Altered form of French Labbé ‘the priest, the abbot’ (see Labbe ).

variants.name: Labay
meanings: Altered form of French Labbé ‘the priest, the abbot’ (see Labbe ).
Americanized form of Czech and Slovak Labaj or Lábaj: nickname derived from the dialect verb lábat ‘to swig’.

variants.name: Abbe
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Abbe, which is usually a pet form of the female name Aubrey but may sometimes be a pet form of a male name beginning with Ab-, namely Abel , Abraham or Absalom . This surname is rare in Britain.
English: occasionally perhaps also a variant of   Abbey  .
German: from a pet form of the personal name Albrecht (see Albert ).

variants.name: Labat
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Chanel, Germain, Lucien, Michel, Monique, Ulysse.
French (southern): topographic name for someone who lived in a valley, from Gascon dialect bat ‘valley’, with fused feminine definite article la.
French (southern): nickname from Occitan abat ‘priest, abbot’ (see Labbe ), with fused definite article l'.

nameMeanings.name: Hervé
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally Breton. See Harvey .

nameMeanings.name: Ier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and Norwegian vernacular form of   Peter .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: form of the name of the best-known of all Christ's apostles, traditionally regarded as the founder of the Christian Church. The name derives, via Latin, from Greek petros ‘stone, rock’. This is used as a translation of the Aramaic byname Cephas, given to the apostle Simon son of Jona, to distinguish him from another of the same name ( Simon Zelotes ). ‘When Jesus beheld him, he said, Thou art Simon the son of Jona: thou shalt be called Cephas, which is by interpretation, A stone’ (John 1:42). According to Matthew 16:17–18, Christ says more explicitly, ‘Blessed art thou, Simon Bar-jona … thou art Peter, and upon this rock I will build my church’. The name has been in continuous use since the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE was born about 1190, in Finistère, Brittany, France, his father, Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé, was 27 and his mother, Marguerite de Saint Tudy, was 21. He had at least 1 son with de GUEMENE. He died about 1225, at the age of 37.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE est né environ 1190, à Finistère, Bretagne, France, son père, Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Marguerite de Saint Tudy, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec de GUEMENE. Il est décédé environ 1225, à l’âge de 37 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE um 1190 in Finistère, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé 27 und seine Mutter Marguerite de Saint Tudy 21. Er hatte mit de GUEMENE mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1225 im Alter von 37 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE è nato il circa 1190 nel Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, il suo padre, Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Marguerite de Saint Tudy, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con de GUEMENE. Morì circa 1225, all'età di 37 anni.

ja.Finistère, Bretagne, France: フランスブレタグンフィニストeア
ja.lifeSummary: Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBEが約1190年にフランスブレタグンフィニストeアで生まれた時，父Eudon du Pont-l'Abbéは27歳，母Marguerite de Saint Tudyは21歳であった。彼はde GUEMENEとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は約1225年に37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE(이)가 대략 1190년 프랑스 브레타그니 핑니스트e르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Marguerite de Saint Tudy(은)는 21세였다. 그는 de GUEMENE(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1225년 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

mg.environ 1190–environ 1225: tany ho any amin'ny 1190–tany ho any amin'ny 1225

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE nasceu aproximadamente 1190, em Finistère, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Marguerite de Saint Tudy, tinha 21 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com de GUEMENE. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1225, com 37 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE родился около 1190 г. в местности финистeр, бретагн, Франция, его отцу по имени Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Marguerite de Saint Tudy, 21. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от de GUEMENE. Он умер около 1225 г. в возрасте 37 лет.

sm.environ 1190–environ 1225: e tusa o le 1190–e tusa o le 1225

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hervé Ier du PONT L'ABBE nació aproximadamente 1190, en Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Eudon du Pont-l'Abbé, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Marguerite de Saint Tudy, tenía 21 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con de GUEMENE. Murió aproximadamente 1225, a la edad de 37 años.
